title: geranium pratense var. erianthum
intro: Geranium pratense var. erianthum, also known as the meadow geranium, is a perennial plant species within the geranium family, characterized by its pink to purple flowers and fragrant foliage. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of meadow geranium include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, alleviate menstrual cramps, and soothe skin irritations, among other uses. Herbal preparations of meadow geranium include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
